checks.question: Does the implementation match the accepted final artifact and owner?
Are product facts, metrics, assets, and proof real or honestly labeled placeholders?
Does every external asset have a verified source, author or owner, license or permission, checksum, and publication-compatible use?
Can the primary user complete the intended workflow end to end?
Does the target artifact open without blocking console, network, or render errors?
Is the first scan order clear through structure, scale, grouping, and contrast?
Does density, composition, and interaction fit the actual product domain?
Do 320, 375, 414, 768, and desktop widths avoid overlap, clipping, and incoherent collapse or fallback?
Do long labels, values, headings, mixed scripts, and localized text fit their containers?
Is keyboard order logical and focus visible, contained, and restored?
Do text, controls, focus, and status pass contrast and avoid color-only meaning?
Are loading, empty, error, success, disabled, and selected states implemented where applicable?
Are metric definitions, units, periods, sources, freshness, and axes honest?
Are every statistic, scale, unit, annotation, uncertainty, legend, and source accurate and readable at distribution size?
Does every frame have one dominant idea and does the sequence form a coherent argument?
Is native PowerPoint work routed rather than represented as equivalent HTML conversion?
Was editable versus fidelity mode explicitly selected before the HTML deck was implemented?
Do all slides pass DOM, geometry, overflow, font, image, and unsupported-CSS validation before any PPTX is written?
Are text, shapes, lists, and images represented as editable PowerPoint objects rather than slide screenshots?
Is image-backed non-editability explicit and does every slide match the approved HTML render?
Are repeated color, type, spacing, radius, shadow, and motion values semantic and consistent?
Does the result have a content-specific visual idea instead of default template composition?
Is every card an independent item rather than a default wrapper or nested surface?
Does motion explain state or narrative, remain interruptible, and respect reduced motion?
Do critical fonts, images, charts, and canvases render before acceptance capture?
Does the editable source render at exact target geometry without console, network, font, image, SVG, Canvas, or WebGL failure?
Are identity, message, and product proof legible both at full resolution and realistic distribution size?
Does the asset show current product truth rather than a generic or stale visual?
Does a verified static poster communicate the core message when motion cannot play?
Can every frame be reproduced through a deterministic time setter after all assets are ready?
Are first, middle, poster, and final frames nonblank, unclipped, readable, and free from black flashes or unintended jumps?
Do codec, dimensions, duration, frame rate, loop or silence policy, and file size match the accepted contract?
When transparency is required, does the encoded stream retain alpha and composite cleanly over contrasting backgrounds?
Does the GIF loop as accepted, remain readable at distribution size, and stay within its file budget?
Do page count, dimensions, searchable text, fonts, and print backgrounds match the HTML source?
Was the artifact checked against the structured anti-template rules, with any matching trap removed or justified by product truth?
Does an arbitrary deterministic seek render the same frame without CSS transitions, stale tween state, or first-frame proxy gaps?
Are font and media warmup completed in the capture context without leaking warmup frames into the encoded output?
Do local, file, and served delivery modes resolve assets without hidden CORS, path, or remote-CDN dependence?
Are geometry and line breaks measured only after the accepted fonts are ready, with a tested fallback path?
Can each scene or state reset independently without inheriting color, transform, opacity, time, or interaction residue?
Did the weighted critique pass its blockers and concept-quality veto instead of averaging away a fundamental failure?
Are target viewport or frame renders, exercised interactions, and test results recorded?
